arts.ttl: Barcelona president says club hounded ‘by land, air and sea’ over Olmo saga
arts.sum: Joan Laporta says enemies hoped to ‘destabilise’ the club President defends ‘contained euphoria’ before Super Cup Joan Laporta denounced an attack on Barcelona “by land, air and sea” and insisted there was no reason why he should resign as he spoke publicly for the first time since the club were allowed to register Dani Olmo and Pau Víctor in defiance of the ruling by La Liga and the country’s football federation. The Barcelona president claimed the club had correctly presented “all” the documentation needed to register Olmo and Víctor by the 31 December deadline but admitted they had not been able to provide the proof of income to comply with financial controls until 3 January. He alleged that was through no fault of their own and that the league made unexpected and unnecessary demands of Barcelona, hinting that the federation (RFEF) had said the registration should have been completed. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Flick talks Araujo and Ansu Fati futures
arts.sum: As we enter the final two weeks of the January transfer window, two Barcelona players are being heavily linked with an exit: Ronald Araujo is seriously thinking about leaving the club and has an offer from Juventus on the table, while Ansu Fati is clearly not part of Hansi Flick’s plans and is attracting interest from a few clubs in Spain and around Europe. I said in the summer that it was up to him to give his best and that’s what happened, although he hasn’t been able to show us 100% of how good he is, and it’s a shame.

arts.ttl: Barca's Olmo granted temporary permission to play
arts.sum: Forwards Dani Olmo and Pau Victor will be allowed to play for Barcelona after the club was granted provisional permission to register the pair by the Spanish national sports council (CSD).

arts.ttl: Laporta talks Omo saga, Araujo future, Nike deal, Camp Nou return
arts.sum: The president also spoke about the club's new kit deal, selling off VIP boxes, returning to Camp Nou and the future of defender Ronald Araujo . Laporta discussed the recent mess regarding the registrations of Dani Olmo and Pau Victor that saw both players temporarily sidelined.
